rulings.Case Title: CONCHITA VILLAROMAN CLARK ET AL VS. RICHARD VILLAROMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Richard Villaroman And Lisa Stone For A Protective Order And For Imposition Of Sanctions Under C.C.P. Sections 1987.1 And 2025.420
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTs RICHARD VILLAROMAN and LISA STONE's Motion For A Protective Order And For Imposition Of Sanctions Under C.C.P. Sections 1987.1 And 2025.420 Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS RICHARD VILLAROMAN AND LISA STONE'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NO. 6 OF THE ACADEMY MORTGAGE SUBPOENA AND REQUESTS NO. 5, 6 & 7 OF THE KPIX SUBPOENA ARE SUSTAINED. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM, AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 25, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: K1 INTERNATIONAL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA VS. BRIDGE INTERNATIONAL MARKETING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CHRISTINA YANG'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant Yang's motion to vacate default and default judgment is granted pursuant to CCP 473.5. The default and default judgment are not void under CCP 473(d) because plaintiff properly substitute served the person "apparently in charge" of Yang's office within the meaning of CCP 415.20(b). Given the liberal policy to hear cases on the merits, Yang's declaration sufficiently establishes that she lacked actual knowledge of the lawsuit in time to defend and did not avoid service. The court denies plaintiff's request for fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: K1 INTERNATIONAL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA VS. BRIDGE INTERNATIONAL MARKETING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS BRIDGE INTERNATIONAL MARKETING, INC., PIONEER PRODUCE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant Bridge International Marketing, Inc. and Pioneer Product, Inc.'s motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied. The default and default judgment are not void under CCP 473(d) because plaintiff properly substitute served the person "apparently in charge" of Bridge and Pioneer's office within the meaning of CCP 415.20(b). Bridge and Pioneer fail to show that the default and default judgment were the result of a lack of actual notice. They also fail to show that they are entitled to equitable relief or that their due process rights have been violated. The evidence shows that Bridge and Pioneer's President (Summer Wang) was aware of the lawsuit on the date of service (9/14/17) and even texted with plaintiff's president regarding the lawsuit on that date. (Oliff Dec., 10-15; Ex. BB.) Wang also fails to explain why or how plaintiff's numerous mailings regarding the lawsuit did not reach her. The court does not find Wang's declaration credible and therefore the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT SUMMER WANG'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment. Defendant Wang's motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied. The default and default judgment are not void under CCP 473(d) because plaintiff properly substitute served the person "apparently in charge" of Wang's office within the meaning of CCP 415.20(b). Wang fails to show that the default and default judgment were the result of a lack of actual notice. She also fails to show that she is entitled to equitable relief or that her due process rights have been violated. The evidence shows that Wang was aware of the lawsuit on the date of service (9/14/17) and even texted with plaintiff's president regarding the lawsuit on that date. (Oliff Dec., 10-15; Ex. BB.) Wang also fails to explain why or how plaintiff's numerous mailings regarding the lawsuit did not reach her. The court does not find Wang's declaration credible and therefore the motion is denied. rulings.Case Title: MELISA OLIVERO VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Department Of Health To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production And Privilege Log
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MELISA OLIVERO's Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Department Of Health To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production And Privilege Log Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FOR DEFENDANT TO HER SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND PRIVILEGE LOG IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 17, 18, 19, 37, 47 & 50 ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT WILL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WITHHOLDING ONLY THOSE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO WHICH IT IS ASSERTING A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY/CLIENT OR WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. SUCH DOCUMENTS WILL BE IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG WITH SUFFICIENT DETAIL TO ALLOW A CHALLENGE TO SUCH DESIGNATION. 2. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS AS TO REQUESTS NOS. 23, 24, 25 & 26 ARE SUSTAINED AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED. 3. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 22 IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WILL SUPPLEMENT ITS SPECIAL INTERROGATORY LOG TO IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS TO WHICH IT MAKES RERERENCE IN ITS RESPONSE. 4. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 25 IS GRANTED; DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANT WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WILL PROVIDE A FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSE TO THE INTERROGATORY. 5. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 26, 27, 28, 29, 32, 33, 37, 94-100 ARE SUSTAINED AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED. 6. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO AMEND ITS PRIVILEGE LOG IS DENIED. 7. NO MONTETARY SANSCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED. rulings.Case Title: ENDOVENTION, INC. ET AL VS. CONFLUENT MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS BRICE MENARDIERE, CLAY BALDWIN, JOSEF FRIEDMANN, CONFLUENT MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC., CORPUS MEDICAL HOLDINGS, INC., CORPUS MEDICAL, LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrers are OVERRULED. Contrary to defendants' arguments, breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing sounds in contract. (1 Witkin, Summary of California Law (2018) §824.) Moreover, the implied covenant may be pled in the alternative to breach of contract, as it adequately has been here. (See Racine v. Laramie, Ltd. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 1026, 1031-32 (conduct "not technically transgressing the express covenants"); Cmplt. 18-20, 22-23, 27, 29-30, 42, 46-47, 50, 53-54.) The fraud claims are pled with adequate specificity, including facts from which intent not to perform can be inferred. (Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30-31.) Defendants argue the facts, which is inappropriate for demurrers. Individual defendants may be liable for their misrepresentations made on a company's behalf. (Ashai Kasei Pharm Corp. v. Actelion Ltd. 2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945, 966.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
